M
Home·The House

A house with stories

We didn't set out to run a guesthouse. We set out to save an old house — and couldn't bear to keep it to ourselves.

Your hosts

Hannah & Pieter,
and a very old kitchen

We found the parsonage in 2019 — pressed ceilings sagging, a fig tree gone wild, and a kitchen built for feeding a congregation. We've been feeding people in it ever since.

Hannah cooks; Pieter pours the coffee and knows every walk in the Swartberg. Between us we keep five rooms, a garden, two hens and a dog called Biscuit. It's just us — which is rather the point.

— see you at breakfast
the two of us, 2019
how we do things

Four house rules we keep

Breakfast comes first

Made from scratch, from the garden where we can. The heart of the house, not an add-on.

Stay small

Five rooms is exactly enough to know your name and still get the bread out on time.

Gentle on the village

Solar where we can, water we're careful with, everything bought down the road.

Honest pricing

Book direct and pay what you see. No agents, no surcharges, no fee at checkout.

1873
The old parsonage
5
Rooms, all different
2
Hens (and Biscuit)
4.97
From 240 stays
around the house

Bits and pieces of home

the sitting room
the garden
breakfast
the herb beds
come for breakfast

There's a room with your name on it

Well — once you've booked. Five rooms, one very good breakfast, booked direct with the house.

See the roomsSay hello
MMarmalade House

A five-room guesthouse in a restored 1873 parsonage in Prince Albert, in the heart of the Karoo. Sunny rooms, a fig garden, and very good breakfast.

The house

Stay
Book a roomGetting hereFAQ
Notes from the kitchen

Open dates, the odd recipe, and when the figs are ripe. Once a month, never more.
